headlines.title: British Billionaire Slams Europe's Energy Policy and Expands U.S. Oil Business
headlines.summary: Europe and the UK’s approach to energy policy is “all over the place” and eroding the region’s security and growth prospects, Jim Ratcliffe has said in an announcement confirming Ineos’s plans to expand its oil and gas operations in the Gulf of Mexico. headlines.title: EIA: US Crude Oil Inventories Continue to Fall
headlines.summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States decreased by 2.3 million barrels during the week ending May 1, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. The decrease brings commercial stockpiles to 457.2 million barrels, accordi

headlines.title: Lufthansa Warns Strait of Hormuz Closure Will Add $2 Billion in Fuel Costs
headlines.summary: Lufthansa Group expects the surge in jet fuel prices to cost it an additional $2 billion this year as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz “is leading to a shortage in kerosene supply and thus to a significant increase in kerosene prices,” Europe’s biggest airline said on Wednesda

headlines.title: Average U.S. Gasoline Price Tops $4.50 to Near Four-Year High
headlines.summary: The national average price of gasoline in the United States has just topped $4.50 per gallon, the highest level since the middle of July 2022, according to data from GasBuddy, as the Iran war drains fuel inventories and sends crude prices higher.
